Title: Eight relief centres for Kampung Bahagia fire evacuees close
Article:
SANDAKAN: Eight temporary relief centres housing evacuees from the Kampung Bahagia fire here were closed today.
District disaster management committee chairman Datuk Walter Kenson said the decision was made following an agreement reached at a special meeting on the fire disaster held yesterday.
"The closure, effective 1pm today, involves centres at the Taman Batu Sapi People's Housing Project Hall, Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Gas, Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Bahagia, Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Batu Sapi, Dewan Serbaguna Sibuga, Dewan Serbaguna Warga Tua and the Sim-Sim futsal court," he said in a statement.
He also expressed appreciation to all parties for their cooperation and commitment throughout the centres' operations.
Thousands of residents were affected by a fire that destroyed about 1,000 stilt houses in Kampung Bahagia on April 19.
–Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Puteri Umno invites Syed Saddiq, Bella Astillah to join Rumah Bangsa
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Puteri Umno has extended an invitation to Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and his fiancée, singer Bella Astillah, to join the party's Rumah Bangsa initiative.
Puteri Umno chief Datuk Nurul Amal Mohd Fauzi said Rumah Bangsa, initiated under Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi's leadership, aims to foster reconciliation, openness and unity.
Speaking at Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur here today, Nurul Amal said Rumah Bangsa is open to all without favouritism.
"Rumah Bangsa is open to all, and we welcome anyone who wishes to be part of it, without favouritism.
"Even YB Muar (Syed Saddiq) and his fiancée Bella can join us," she said in her speech.
Syed Saddiq, a former Muda president, stepped down from the
post
in November 2023, saying he had no regrets over the decision. He remains a member of the party.
He has also opted not to contest in Muda's 2026 party
elections
, effectively ruling himself out of the race for its top leadership posts.
Away from politics, Syed Saddiq and Bella have drawn public attention over their relationship in recent months. The couple were officially
engaged
on March 28 in a ceremony held in the capital.
Meanwhile, Nurul Amal said Rumah Bangsa reflects Umno's readiness to bring people together, including those who have taken different political paths.
"Rumah Bangsa shows that Umno can be a bridge that unites. A good intention, if managed well, InsyaAllah, will bring good results," she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman jumps from moving e-hailing car in Klang over safety fears
Article:
KLANG: A woman jumped out of a moving e-hailing car along a road near Taman Eng Ann here yesterday morning, allegedly out of fear for her safety.
North Klang police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the incident occurred about 9am and was reported by a 47-year-old local e-hailing driver.
"The driver was transporting two passengers when one of them, a woman seated at the rear right, suddenly opened the door and jumped out while the vehicle was still in motion," he said in a statement.
He said the driver immediately stopped the car and found that the woman had sustained minor injuries.
"The passenger claimed she acted out of anxiety and concern for her safety, alleging that the driver had been observing her through the rear-view mirror without clear reason.
"Following the incident, the driver cancelled the trip and, with the help of a motorcyclist at the scene, the woman was taken to a nearby clinic for treatment," he said.
Vijaya Rao said the driver later lodged a police report as a precautionary measure.
"The case is being investigated under Section 336 of the Penal Code for an act endangering the life or personal safety of others.
"Efforts are ongoing to trace the passenger to assist in the investigation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Starmer says some pro-Palestinian marches could be banned
Article:
LONDON: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has said banning some pro-Palestinian marches could be justified, particularly where chants call for the spread of the "intifada".
Starmer is facing pressure to act following a series of antisemitic incidents, including a stabbing this week in Golders Green, north London, an area with a large Jewish community.
A 45-year-old British man, born in Somalia, has been remanded in custody after appearing in court on Friday charged with attempted murder.
The prime minister visited the scene and a Jewish volunteer ambulance service on Thursday, where he was met with boos from some residents who accused him of not doing enough to protect the community.
They also criticised ongoing pro-Palestinian marches in British cities, which began after Hamas's October 7, 2023 attack on Israel that triggered the war in Gaza.
"I'm a big defender of freedom of expression and peaceful protest," Starmer told the BBC. "But when there are chants like 'globalise the intifada', that is completely off limits."
He said tougher action was needed and that authorities should more strictly police language used during demonstrations.
Starmer added that there were instances where protests should be halted altogether, with discussions ongoing with police on further measures.
The term "intifada" refers to Palestinian uprisings against Israel in 1987–1993 and the early 2000s.
Police in London and Manchester had earlier warned they would arrest anyone chanting "globalise the intifada".
On Thursday, the United Kingdom raised its national security threat level to "severe", citing the Golders Green attack as well as concerns over Islamist extremism and far-right threats.
Police said they would closely assess all future protest-related incidents.
–AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran executes two men convicted of spying for Israel
Article:
TEHRAN: Iran hanged two men on Saturday convicted of spying for Israel, the judiciary said.
The judiciary's Mizan Online website said Yaghoub Karimpour and Nasser Bekrzadeh were hanged for "intelligence cooperation and espionage in favour of the Zionist regime".
It was not immediately clear when the two men were arrested.
Mizan said Karimpour was convicted of the capital offence of "moharebeh," or waging war against God, for filming and photographing security and military locations.
The website said he sent the material to a Mossad officer during the 12-day war with Israel in June 2025.
The judiciary added that Bekrzadeh worked with Mossad by sending information on religious and provincial figures, as well as important centres such as the Natanz nuclear site.
Mizan did not specify whether Bekrzadeh's activities took place during the war.
On Feb 28, Israel and the United States launched strikes on Iran, triggering another war that has been paused since April 8 under a fragile ceasefire.
Iran has recently carried out multiple executions of people linked to mass protests in January. Authorities said the protests were instigated by Israel, the US, and opposition groups, including the banned People's Mujahedin organisation.
On Thursday, Iran said it hanged Sasan Azadvar, who was convicted of attacking police officers in Isfahan province during the pre-war protests.
The demonstrations began in late December over rising living costs before spreading nationwide and evolving into anti-government protests that peaked on Jan 8 and 9.
Iranian authorities said the rallies began peacefully before turning into "foreign-instigated riots" involving killings and vandalism.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Humpback whale stranded in Germany released into North Sea
Article:
FRANKFURT: A humpback whale that had been struggling to survive after beaching near the German coast was today released into the North Sea off Denmark after being transported in a barge, a member of a rescue mission said.
Dubbed "Timmy" by the German media, the whale was first spotted stuck on a sandbank on March 23 near the city of Luebeck before freeing itself and then becoming stuck again several times.
The whale left the barge it had been towed on from Wismar Bay on the Baltic coast at around 8:45am (0645 GMT), said Karin Walter-Mommert from the rescue initiative.
It is now swimming on its own and freely, and at least for the time being, in the right direction, she said.
At the start of April, German officials gave up on trying to rescue the animal, saying they believed it could not be saved.
But this triggered an outcry and authorities were persuaded to approve a privately financed rescue plan proposed by two wealthy entrepreneurs.
The barge idea was hatched after their initial attempt to save the whale with inflatable cushions and pontoons was unsuccessful.
The rescue effort was seen as a long shot and criticised by experts who said it would only cause the animal more distress.
The whale's ordeal has sparked a media frenzy – with non-stop coverage from TV channels, online outlets and social media influencers – but has also led to angry spats and conspiracy theories. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Umno proposes 'Rumah Generasi Baru' initiative to win over young voters
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno is looking to institutionalise youth engagement ahead of upcoming elections, with Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh proposing the establishment of a "Rumah Generasi Baru" under the party's broader Rumah Bangsa framework.
Speaking at Umno's 80th anniversary convention at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur, Rauf said the initiative is aimed at ensuring leadership continuity and strengthening the party's long-term support base.
"I wish to call upon the president to establish a Rumah Generasi Baru under Rumah Bangsa, so that the coming generation can carry forward the party's mission," he said.
The proposal comes as Umno grapples with the impact of an expanded voter base following the implementation of automatic voter registration and the lowering of the voting age to 18.
Rauf said the shift has disrupted the party's traditional grassroots intelligence systems, limiting its ability to effectively track and engage new voters.
"We used to rely on ground intelligence to reach voters whose origins we already knew. But after 18-year-olds were automatically registered, we could no longer track new voters," he said.
He said that while BN secured a strong victory in the Melaka state election, the coalition failed to translate that success at the parliamentary level — a gap he attributed partly to the changing voter landscape.
In response, Rauf said Melaka has pioneered a "Digital JR (Jentera Rakyat)" approach, leveraging social media and mobilising party machinery to better reach younger voters.
"Thinking has changed. The direction has changed. Melaka has become the pioneer of Digital JR — a medium through which we can reach young voters," he said.
Rauf also linked the push for generational renewal to the party's broader electoral outlook, noting that Melaka faces a state election within months and must serve as a model for Umno's resurgence.
"I will make Melaka a key battleground as we head into the state election, which is now only 150 days away," he said.
Although the date has yet to be fixed, the Melaka state election is due by November 2026, with polls widely expected before the term expires.
Rauf added that Umno must ensure it does not lose ground again, warning that the party cannot afford to be sidelined on its own turf if it fails to rebuild support among the next generation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran announces new maritime rules amid Strait of Hormuz tensions
Article:
ISTANBUL: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has announced "new rules" governing the country's coastline along the Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz, amid heightened regional tensions.
The move was made at the directive of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, Anadolu Ajansi reported, citing a local broadcaster.
The IRGC Navy said it would exercise control over nearly 2,000 kilometres of Iran's coastline in the affected areas, describing the waters as both a "source of livelihood and strength" for Iranians and a contributor to regional security and prosperity, according to state-run Press TV.
No further details were disclosed.
Tensions have escalated in the Strait of Hormuz after Tehran restricted passage through the strategic waterway in response to US-Israeli strikes that began on February 28.
A ceasefire was announced on April 8 through Pakistani mediation, followed by talks in Islamabad on April 11 and 12, but no agreement was reached.
US President Donald Trump later unilaterally extended the truce without setting a new timeframe, at Pakistan's request.
Since April 13, the United States has enforced a naval blockade targeting Iranian maritime traffic in the waterway.
–BERNAMA-ANADOLU
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy rain, strong winds expected as MetMalaysia issues nationwide alert
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for multiple states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to persist until 6pm today.
In a statement issued at 3.35pm, MetMalaysia said the warning covers Perlis, Kedah and Penang, as well as several districts in Perak, including Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim.
The department said similar conditions are forecast in Kelantan, involving Pasir Mas, Jeli, Tanah Merah, Machang, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang, as well as Terengganu, covering Besut, Setiu, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman.
In Pahang, the affected areas include Lipis, Jerantut, Maran, Kuantan, Bera and Rompin, while the warning also extends to Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.
MetMalaysia added that several areas in Negri Sembilan, namely Seremban, Port Dickson and Rembau, as well as Melaka and parts of Johor — Segamat, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Pontian and Kulai — are also expected to experience similar conditions.
In Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching, Samarahan, Betong (Saratok), Sarikei, Sibu, Mukah, Kapit, Bintulu, Miri and Limbang.
In Sabah, thunderstorms are expected in the Interior districts of Sipitang, Kuala Penyu and Beaufort, as well as the West Coast areas of Papar, Putatan, Penampang, Kota Kinabalu, Tuaran and Kota Belud, and parts of Kudat including Kota Marudu and Pitas.
The warning also applies to Labuan.
MetMalaysia said the alert is issued when there are signs of thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20mm per hour, which are either imminent or expected to last for more than an hour.
"Thunderstorm warnings are short-term alerts, valid for a period not exceeding 6 hours for each issuance," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran military official says renewed war with US 'likely'
Article:
TEHRAN: A senior Iranian military official has said renewed fighting between Iran and the United States is "likely", amid renewed tension over stalled nuclear negotiations.
The remarks came hours after US President Donald Trump said he was "not satisfied" with a new Iranian negotiating proposal delivered to mediator Pakistan.
Iran reportedly submitted the draft on Thursday evening, though its contents were not disclosed by state media.
Trump said he was unhappy with progress in talks, citing "tremendous discord" within Iran's leadership, and suggested military action remained an option, though he said he would "prefer not" to take it.
"A renewed conflict between Iran and the United States is likely," said Mohammad Jafar Asadi, a senior figure in Iran's central military command, according to the Fars news agency.
He added that the United States was "not committed to any promises or agreements".
Iran's judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei said Tehran had "never shied away from negotiations" but would not accept imposed peace terms.
The White House has not detailed the latest Iranian proposal, though reports said US envoy Steve Witkoff had submitted amendments linked to Iran's nuclear programme.
Oil prices briefly fell on the news but remain elevated amid ongoing disruption to the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping route.
The conflict, which began in late February and has been on hold since April 8, has also triggered wider regional instability, including continued strikes in Lebanon despite a separate truce with Hezbollah.
In Iran, economic pressure is mounting, with inflation rising and sanctions tightening.
"There has been no exchange of fire between United States Forces and Iran since April 7, 2026," Trump said in a letter to Congress, adding that hostilities "have terminated".
–AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says 'not satisfied' with new Iran proposal
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Friday he was "not satisfied" with a new Iranian negotiating proposal, as peace talks remain stalled despite a weeks-long ceasefire.
Iran delivered the draft to mediator Pakistan on Thursday evening, the IRNA news agency reported, without disclosing its contents.
"At this moment I'm not satisfied with what they're offering," Trump told reporters, blaming the deadlock on "tremendous discord" within Iran's leadership.
"Do we want to go and just blast the hell out of them and finish them forever – or do we want to try and make a deal?" he said, adding he would "prefer not" to take military action "on a human basis".
The war, launched by the United States and Israel with surprise strikes on February 28, has been on hold since April 8, with only one failed round of direct talks since then.
Trump, under domestic pressure over congressional authorisation, wrote to lawmakers declaring hostilities "terminated", despite no change in US military posture.
The White House has not detailed the latest Iranian proposal, though reports said US envoy Steve Witkoff had submitted amendments placing Tehran's nuclear programme back on the negotiating table.
Iran has maintained control over the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting global oil and gas flows, while the United States has imposed counter-measures on Iranian ports.
Despite the ceasefire, tensions remain high across the region, with continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon despite a separate truce with Hezbollah.
Lebanon's health ministry said 13 people were killed in southern strikes, including in Habboush, where evacuation orders were issued shortly before the attack.
Washington also announced major arms sales to Middle East allies, including a US$4 billion Patriot missile deal with Qatar and nearly US$1 billion in weapons systems to Israel.
Iran's judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni Ejei said Tehran had "never shied away from negotiations" but would not accept imposed terms.
Oil prices briefly fell on news of the Iranian proposal but remain elevated amid the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
Tehran resident Amir told AFP the situation "feels like we are stuck in purgatory", adding he expected further escalation.
"This is all to waste time," he said, predicting renewed strikes by the United States and Israel.
Washington is also facing legal and political scrutiny over whether Trump must seek congressional approval to continue the conflict.
"There has been no exchange of fire between United States Forces and Iran since April 7, 2026," Trump said, adding that hostilities "have terminated".
– AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Children seen sticking heads out of sunroof in viral video sparks concern
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two children were seen putting themselves at risk after they were seen sticking their heads out of a moving vehicle's sunroof, in a video that has sparked concern on social media.
The footage, uploaded on X by user @update11111, showed an Omoda car stopped at a traffic light.
Two children stood inside the vehicle with their heads protruding through the sunroof.
As the traffic light turned green, a girl sat back down inside the car.
A boy, however, continued to keep his head out of the sunroof as the vehicle moved off.
The post carried a caption urging parents and road users to be responsible and not endanger children's lives.
"Be responsible parents and road users. Do not endanger your children's lives," the caption read.
Netizens in the thread tagged the Road Transport Department and police, calling for action against those involved.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wild elephants destroy pineapple farm in Jerantut causing RM20k losses
Article:
JERANTUT: A pineapple farmer suffered losses of nearly RM20,000 after his farm was damaged by a herd of wild elephants at the Lembah Gelanggi Agricultural Project in Pulau Tawar near here today.
Zainul Ismail, 68, said nearly 50 MD2 pineapple plants were damaged in the incident, believed to have occurred between 3am and 6am.
"Based on the traces found, it is believed that about 10 elephants entered the farm early this morning.
"The elephants are believed to have come from the Lepar Forest Reserve before taking shelter in forest fringe areas near Felda Kota Gelanggi and Kampung Bukit Rang in search of food," he said when met.
He said the MD2 pineapple plants had already started producing yield, with some of the produce marketed in Jerantut and to traders in the Klang Valley.
"When I arrived at the farm this morning, I found many pineapple plants uprooted, while both mature and young fruits were scattered on the ground and damaged.
"The 20ha farm has about 100,000 pineapple plants and part of the harvest has already been marketed," he said.
Zainul said the incident was the third in five years, with total losses exceeding RM100,000.
"My main concern is the newly initiated MS16 pineapple project, as this variety has high value and commands a premium price in the market.
"I have lodged a report with the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) so that immediate action can be taken to control the threat posed by these wild animals," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SME group hails Rain Rave festival as key economic driver
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Rain Rave 2026 Water Music Festival is a timely boost for small and medium enterprises amid mounting economic pressures, the SME Association of Malaysia said.
Its president, Dr Chin Chee Seong, said large-scale, internationally appealing events such as the festival go beyond festive celebrations and serve as key economic drivers for businesses facing slower growth, rising costs and an increasingly uncertain operating environment.
The Rain Rave 2026 Water Music Festival was held in Bukit Bintang, Kuala Lumpur from April 30 to May 2, 2026.
Chin said Bukit Bintang, one of the country's main tourism and commercial hubs, provides an ideal platform to showcase Malaysia's diversity, creativity and hospitality.
"Events of this nature contribute to increased footfall, higher tourist spending and the creation of immediate business opportunities for SMEs across sectors including retail, food and beverage, hospitality, transportation and services," he said in a statement.
He added that such initiatives create strong multiplier effects across the SME ecosystem, with higher visitor traffic translating into improved sales, stronger cash flow and better business sentiment.
"These are critically needed as SMEs continue to navigate cost pressures, demand volatility and global economic headwinds," he said.
Such events, he said, also reinforce national identity while enhancing Malaysia's image as a vibrant and welcoming destination in an increasingly competitive regional tourism landscape.
The festival, organised by the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry, was also seen as a strategic effort to position the country as a preferred destination for unique cultural experiences.
Chin urged the government to continue promoting and expanding similar initiatives, while calling for stronger collaboration between government agencies, industry players and SME stakeholders.
"This is to ensure that more local businesses can participate in, benefit from and grow through such initiatives," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Boy killed in 3-vehicle crash in Kampar, Hilux driver arrested for alleged drug use
Article:
KAMPAR: A 10-year-old boy was killed and three others injured in a crash involving a Toyota Hilux and two cars here last night.
Police said the Hilux driver tested positive for cannabis after the collision at KM29.4 of Jalan Ipoh–Kuala Lumpur.
The victim has been identified as Jayden Leong Zhi Yung from Taman Perpaduan Koperasi, Ipoh.
District police chief Superintendent Mohamad Nazri Daud said the incident occurred at 10.35pm after police received a public alert on a collision involving the three vehicles.
He said the Toyota Hilux, travelling from Ipoh towards Kampar, is believed to have lost control before veering into the opposite lane and colliding with a Perodua Bezza and a Perodua Myvi.
"The impact caused severe damage to all three vehicles," he said.
Mohamad Nazri said the boy, a front passenger in the Bezza, died at the scene from head and leg injuries, while a 13-year-old rear passenger and the 46-year-old driver were injured and taken to Kampar Hospital.
He said the Myvi driver, 23, and the Hilux driver, 44, were not injured.
"We have arrested the man and the case is being investigated under Section 44 of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless driving causing death, which carries a heavy penalty upon conviction," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan Umno: 'We'd rather be opposition than stay in PH-led govt'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno said it would rather sit as the opposition than remain in the state government following its decision to withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
State chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the party did not want to be tainted by alleged wrongdoing involving the menteri besar in relation to the state's adat and constitutional crisis.
In an unusual move, Umno's 14 assemblymen, who are part of the state unity government,
withdrew support
for the Pakatan Harapan-led menteri besar while continuing to back the government.
"If, after we have withdrawn our support for the menteri besar, he continues to manage the state government, that means we have lost our trust… up to and including the possibility that Umno is prepared to become the opposition in government," he told reporters on the sidelines of Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur.
Jalaluddin said the decision was driven by concerns over governance, claiming the crisis had escalated to a point that affected stability and risked damaging the reputation of the state government and its institutions.
"This is not a position driven by political interests. This is about the governance of a government responsible for managing the institution of adat and the Rulers in Negri Sembilan," he said.
He said Negri Sembilan Umno was now focused on preparing for the 16th general election (GE16) and aimed to win all state seats to regain its dominant position.
"Do not risk Umno's future just to protect friends or any party. I take full responsibility for this crisis and, as chairman of the state Umno liaison committee, I am responsible for ensuring Umno returns to a dominant footing."
Jalaluddin said the identity of any replacement for the menteri besar was not the priority, as such a decision rested with the top leadership of Barisan Nasional and Pakatan Harapan.
Negri Sembilan has 36 state seats. Following the state election in 2023, Barisan Nasional formed the state government together with PH after winning 31 seats.
The current seat composition in the state is as follows:
PH: 17
BN: 14
Perikatan Nasional: 5
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UK PM mulls banning some pro-Palestinian marches
Article:
LONDON: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer said in an interview broadcast on Saturday that banning some pro-Palestinian marches could be justified, especially when they call for the intifada to spread.
Labour leader Starmer is under pressure to act after a spate of antisemitic incidents, including this week, when two men were stabbed in the north London suburb of Golders Green, which is home to a large Jewish community.
A 45-year-old British national who was born in Somalia was remanded in custody when he made his first appearance in court on Friday accused of attempted murder.
Starmer visited the scene of the attacks and a Jewish volunteer ambulance service on Thursday and was booed by some locals, who accused him of not doing enough to protect them.
They also denounced pro-Palestinian activists holding marches in British cities, which began after Hamas's October 7, 2023 attack on Israel that sparked the war in Gaza.
The prime minister, a former human rights lawyer and chief public prosecutor whose wife is of Jewish origin, said many Jewish people had told them they were affected by "the repeat nature" of the protests.
"I'm a big defender of freedom of expression, peaceful protests," he told the BBC. "But when there are chants like 'globalise the intifada', that's completely off limits.
"Clearly, there should be tougher action in relation to that."
The intifada refers to the Palestinian civilian uprisings against Israel in 1987-1993 and the early 2000s.
Starmer said he wanted to police the language used on marches more strongly and that there were "instances" when some protests should be stopped altogether.
Discussions had been taking place with the police for some time about what further action could be taken, he added.
In December last year, police in London and the northwest city of Manchester said they would arrest anyone chanting "globalise the intifada."
The Jewish community in Britain views the chant as "very, very dangerous", said Starmer.
On Thursday, the UK increased its security alert level to "severe" – the second highest – in part because of the attack in Golders Green, as well as the threat from Islamist extremism and the far-right.
The police have said they would look closely at all calls about future protests.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Backing the safety net: Malaysians working in Singapore welcome Perkeso protection
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Malaysians working in Singapore say the government's plan to extend Social Security Organisation (Socso) coverage to citizens employed abroad will minimise their exposure to financial risks.
Akmal Hakim Zainuddin, 35, who has worked at SingPost for six years, said the move would provide social security in the event of accidents, especially for those who commute to the island republic daily.
He said many Malaysians working in Singapore rely solely on employer-provided protection, which varies in terms of benefits and coverage.
The absence of personal insurance or takaful plans also exposes workers and their families to high costs in the event of accidents, disability or death.
"If there is protection from Socso, at least we have a backup from our own country.
"This is important because many of us commute daily, especially motorcyclists who are more exposed to the risk of accidents," he said here today.
Technician Raja Haziq Raja Jaafar, 32, who has been commuting from Permas Jaya to Tuas for six years, shared similar sentiments.
"Some companies provide protection, but usually only during working hours, while others provide none at all.
"If an accident occurs while heading home, the worker has to bear the costs. This is where I see the need for protection like Socso," said Raja Haziq, who rides a motorcycle to work.
Yesterday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced the proposal during the National Labour Day 2026 celebration.
He said the government would amend the Self-Employment Social Security Act to allow coverage for Malaysians working abroad, starting with cross-border workers in Singapore.
The move is expected to benefit more than 400,000 Malaysians who commute to Singapore for work.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dzulkefly: No cuts to critical healthcare services, vows counter-proposal
Article:
SANDAKAN: The Health Ministry has said healthcare services, particularly the supply of medicines and medical devices, should not be affected despite a proposed 10 per cent budget cut for the health sector.
Its minister, Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, said public facilities under the ministry are the backbone of essential healthcare services, with operational needs dependent on government hospitals.
He said the ministry will submit a counter-proposal in the near term, adding that any cost-saving measures should not involve critical areas such as patient care, medicine supply and medical requirements.
"We are a facility that provides essential basic services. For me, matters related to healthcare services, medicine supply and medical devices are almost non-negotiable.
"Do not make any cuts as these are basic requirements in delivering good healthcare services," he said after officiating the Wellness Hub Sandakan here today.
Also present were Sandakan Member of Parliament Vivian Wong Shir Yee and Sabah Health Department director Datuk Dr William Gotulis.
The Finance Ministry had previously proposed an operating expenditure reduction of RM5.4 billion involving the health and higher education sectors as part of efforts to strengthen the country's fiscal position.
Dzulkefly said he did not rule out savings in certain areas such as unfilled vacancies, provided they do not affect public services.
He said the ministry is responsible for ensuring universal health coverage and any decision must take into account the sustainability of the public healthcare system.
On the Wellness Hub, Dzulkefly said the ministry plans to expand the network nationwide with an additional 81 hubs, bringing the total to 119 centres.
"Involvement from various parties, including elected representatives, is encouraged to secure investment or contributions through corporate social responsibility from private companies, conglomerates and industry players.
"The cost of a Wellness Hub is estimated at RM700,000 to RM800,000, but the impact is significant in promoting a healthy lifestyle," he said.
The Wellness Hub in Sandakan is the fifth in Sabah after Putatan, Keningau, Kudat and Lahad Datu, with operations beginning in February.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four teens arrested for assaulting boy, dunking head in bucket
Article:
MELAKA: Police have detained four secondary school students for allegedly bullying a 12-year-old boy at an orphanage in Pantai Puteri, Tanjung Kling.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the victim was reportedly kicked and punched by a group of senior students.
The suspects also allegedly submerged the victim's head in a bucket of water and dragged him across a toilet floor.
The incident came to light after the boy's mother lodged a police report on Wednesday night after learning of the assault.
Acting on the report, police arrested four boys, aged 15 and 16, the following day.
"The suspects have no criminal records and tested negative for drugs," Dzulkhairi said in a statement.
The suspects were released on police bail after their statements were recorded.
The case is being investigated under Section 507B of the Penal Code for bullying, which carries a maximum penalty of three years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two arrested after police chase ends in crash in Subang Jaya [WATCH]
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Two people were arrested after a police chase involving a Proton Preve ended in a crash with a BMW at a traffic light junction in Subang Jaya last night.
The incident has gone viral on social media with clips showing the Proton being chased by multiple police cars.
The incident even got the attention of Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh, who was on her way home when the crash occurred.
Yeoh took a video of the crashed BMW and Proton as police secured the scene at the junction.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said police received a report on the incident at 9.05pm on May 1.
"The chase began along the New Klang Valley Expressway near the Subang toll exit heading towards Shah Alam after the suspects allegedly tried to evade a police inspection.
"The suspect drove dangerously into the Subang Jaya intersection before crashing into a black BMW at the traffic light junction along Persiaran Tujuan, Subang Jaya," he said in a statement today.
He said the suspects were arrested by police at the scene.
"Upon further checks, police found 17 packets containing a substance believed to be drugs weighing about 16g.
"The first suspect, a local man, has a record of 21 criminal and drug-related offences, while the second suspect, a foreign woman, has no priors," he said.
Shamsudin said the man tested positive for methamphetamine while the woman tested negative for drugs.
"Both suspects have been remanded for three days until May 4.
"The case is being investigated under Section 186 of the Penal Code, Section 12(2) of the Dangerous Drugs Act and Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act," he said.
Witnesses can contact the nearest police station or the Petaling Jaya police operations room at 03-7966 2222.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says US Navy acting 'like pirates' to carry out naval blockade of Iranian ports
Article:
WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said on Friday the US Navy was acting "like pirates" in carrying out Washington's naval blockade of Iranian ports during the US and Israel's war against Iran.
Trump made the comments while describing the seizure by US forces of a ship a few days ago.
"We took over the ship, we took over the cargo, we took over the oil. It's a very profitable business," Trump said in remarks on Friday evening. "We're like pirates. We're sort of like pirates but we are not playing games."
Some of Teheran's vessels have been seized by the US after leaving Iranian ports, along with sanctioned container ships and Iranian tankers in Asian waters.
Iran has blocked nearly all ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz apart from its own since the start of the war. Trump has imposed a separate blockade of Iranian ports.
The US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28. Iran responded with its own strikes on Israel and Gulf states that host US bases. US-Israeli strikes on Iran and Israeli attacks in Lebanon have killed thousands and displaced millions.
The war has raised oil prices and led to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a chokepoint for about 20 per cent of global oil and ​liquefied natural gas shipments.
Trump, who has offered shifting timelines and goals for the war that remains unpopular in the US, has faced widespread condemnation over his comments on the conflict, including when he threatened to destroy Iran's entire civilisation last month.
Many US experts said last month that American strikes on Iran may amount to war crimes after Trump threatened to target civilian infrastructure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman, 62, claims loan sharks assaulted her in restaurant over debt
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 62-year-old woman has alleged she was assaulted by loan sharks inside a restaurant owned by them over unpaid debts.
During a press conference held alongside the Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO), the victim, who wishes to be known as Ms Wong, said she had taken out loans to cover medical expenses for her husband, who is battling cancer.
She later claimed she was deceived into signing documents to repay the debt, which resulted in multiple companies and bank accounts being opened under her name, China Press reported.
Although more than a month has passed since the incident, Wong said she still experiences pain in the areas where she was attacked.
Recounting the ordeal, she said that inside a restaurant operated by the alleged loan shark, a young man pulled down the shutters before he and his associates began punching and kicking her.
Her face became severely swollen, and after she collapsed, she was repeatedly kicked in the chest.
"My husband was there at the time, but the assailants restrained him. He could only stand by helplessly, unable to intervene," she said.
She added that the ordeal lasted about two hours before she was finally released. By then, she was too badly injured to walk and had to be carried out by her husband and the loan shark's associates.
"While I was being beaten, I endured the pain without crying. It was only after I was carried out of the restaurant that I broke down in tears.
"I truly do not understand how these young people could be so cruel to an elderly woman," she added.
She has since lodged a police report and provided the authorities with details of the suspects.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] No backing down: 14 Negri Sembilan Umno reps firm on withdrawal of support for MB [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the 14 assemblymen from the party who withdrew confidence from Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will not be reversing their position.
"I have already stated the position of the 14 assemblymen in Negri Sembilan: that we have lost confidence in the leadership of the Menteri Besar of Negri Sembilan.
"This means we will not be shifting our position on this matter," Jalaluddin told reporters on the sidelines of Umno's 80th anniversary celebrations here today.
Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said on Thursday that
all 14 assemblymen had agreed to continue supporting the unity government
in the state.
Zahid said the decision was reached after discussions with the assemblymen.
On whether Umno would support a new Menteri Besar candidate who is also from Pakatan Harapan (PH), Jalaluddin said that decision was not for Umno Negri Sembilan to make.
"That decision rests with the highest leadership, between the Barisan Nasional and Pakatan Harapan leaderships," he said, adding that the party would await whatever the top leadership decided on the matter.
However, Jalaluddin, who is also Jelebu Member of Parliament and Pertang assemblyman, highlighted that the identity of any replacement was secondary to the principle at stake.
"The issue is not about politics, not about power, not about any individual.
"The issue is that a fundamental duty as Menteri Besar is this: you cannot allow a crisis to escalate to the point where an adat crisis erupts in Negri Sembilan," he said.
Asked whether Umno was prepared to sit in opposition if Aminuddin remained in office, Jalaluddin said yes.
"Umno is prepared to become the opposition in government until the next state election or the 16th General Election," he said.
Jalaluddin said the move by the Umno assemblymen was not driven by political ambition or factional manoeuvring.
"This is not a position driven by political interests, by the pursuit of power, or by what people call 'tebuk atap', this is about the governance of a government that is responsible for managing the institution of adat and the Rulers in Negri Sembilan," he said.
He said that the crisis had its roots in mismanagement by the Menteri Besar, which had allowed a constitutional and adat crisis to erupt, one that dragged the royal institution into public controversy.
Jalaluddin warned that the public airing of the adat dispute had already done damage, with social media debates generating conflicting perceptions among the public, particularly among young Malaysians who lacked grounding in the customary traditions of the royal state.
On Tuesday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the
Menteri Besar would continue serving following a decree by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir
.
Anwar said the state's political crisis was being resolved through legal channels. He said Aminuddin had accepted the royal decree and would continue carrying out his duties while unresolved constitutional matters were clarified.
On April 27, Negri Sembilan Umno and BN withdrew support for the state government led by Aminuddin, citing a crisis involving the state's Adat Perpatih customary system and its constitutional framework.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US sanctions are 'collective punishment,' says Cuba during May 1 marches
Article:
HAVANA: Cuba said Friday that US President Donald Trump's fresh sanctions on the island amounted to "collective punishment," as an enormous May 1 procession outside the American embassy in Havana vowed to "defend the homeland."
Trump has mused about taking over Cuba, which lies 145 kilometres from Florida and has been under a nearly continuous US trade embargo since Fidel Castro led a communist revolution in 1959.
Late Friday, he told an audience in Florida that the US would be "taking over" the Caribbean island "almost immediately."
In an executive order on Friday, the US leader said he would
impose sanctions
on people involved in sweeping sections of the Cuban economy, which is steered by the government.
The latest sanctions constitute "collective punishment" of the nation's people, Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez said.
"We firmly reject the recent unilateral coercive measures adopted by the #UnitedStates government," he posted on X in English. "These actions demonstrate an intention to impose, once again, collective punishment on the Cuban people."
In a Spanish-language statement, he called the sanctions "illegal" and "abusive."
Trump's order said the measures target people known to "operate in or have operated in the energy, defence and related materiel, metals and mining, financial services, or security sector of the Cuban economy, or any other sector of the Cuban economy", as well as Cuban officials judged to have engaged in "serious human rights abuses" or corruption.
Already in the throes of economic stagnation, the situation worsened for Cuba following a fuel blockade imposed by Washington in January, with only one Russian oil tanker making it through since then.
Supply shortages and power cuts have become the norm, and tourism – once Cuba's most lucrative industry – has plummeted.
The sanctions come despite moves toward dialogue between the two countries, with senior US officials visiting the island for talks in April.
Friday's fresh measures took effect during May 1 celebrations, which saw huge crowds in Havana march to the US embassy under the slogan "Defend the Homeland." The march was led by Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel and former revolutionary leader Raul Castro.
The day before, Diaz-Canel had called on Cubans to mobilise "against the genocidal blockade and the crude imperial threats to our country," referring to US actions and rhetoric.
During the rally, authorities claimed to have collected more than six million signatures from Cubans "for the homeland and for peace" over the past six weeks.
Opponents, however, have questioned the method by which the signatures were collected.
State television showed thousands more people assembling in other Cuban cities.  - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two new rabies cases detected in Sarawak
Article:
KUCHING: Two new rabies-positive cases have been recorded in Kuching, bringing the cumulative total to 14 cases detected in Sarawak this year.
The latest cases were identified through 41 samples of domestic animals tested between April 27 and May 1.
The Sarawak Veterinary Services Department (JPVS), in a statement on Facebook, said one case was detected in Sungai Tengah Kolong 2 involving a free-roaming pet dog.
Another case was detected in Kampung Sungai Atas Singai, Bau, involving a dog bite incident involving a free-roaming pet dog.
The department said free-roaming domestic dogs are at risk of contracting rabies and may transmit the disease to other animals, posing a threat to human safety.
As such, dog owners are advised to ensure their pets are vaccinated regularly and not allowed to roam freely.
JPVS veterinary officer Dr Ainul Riza Abu Seman said the department continuously conducts free vaccination programmes to encourage more pet owners to vaccinate their animals.
"A dog vaccination programme helps break the chain of infection, which is why we conduct monthly programmes in various local authority areas," she said when met at the Anti-Rabies Vaccination, Licensing and Microchipping Programme at Sungai Maong Market here today.
Dr Ainul said public awareness has been increasing, reflected in the growing number of pet owners bringing their animals for vaccination at JPVS programmes.
"Increased awareness and responsibility can save lives and help achieve a rabies-free community," she said.
For record, since rabies was declared an outbreak in July 2017, a total of 93 human infection cases have been recorded in Sarawak, with 86 fatalities.
Only seven patients survived, most of whom suffered neurological complications.
As of April this year alone, three deaths have been recorded in Sarawak.
END/-
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Canada approves new generic Semaglutide
Article:
OTTAWA, (Canada): Health Canada on Friday authorised a second generic version of the popular diabetes drug semaglutide, further solidifying the country's position as the first G7 nation to approve alternatives to the brand-name drug Ozempic, reported Xinhua.
According to a news release from the federal health agency, the newly approved injection from Canadian-based pharmaceutical company Apotex is indicated for the once-weekly treatment of adult patients with type 2 diabetes to manage blood sugar levels.
The authorisation followed a "thorough review" demonstrating the drug meets Health Canada's criteria for safety, efficacy and quality, said the release.
The move came just three days after Health Canada became the first regulator in a G7 country to approve a generic semaglutide filed by India's Dr. Reddy's Laboratories.
Health Canada said it is currently reviewing seven other submissions from different companies and expects to make more regulatory decisions "in the coming weeks and months."
The availability of generic drugs is expected to have a positive impact in Canada, including potential cost savings for patients and the healthcare system.
In Canada, many generic medications are 45 to 90 per cent cheaper than the brand-name versions, said the release. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Trump is an idiot': Californians fume over soaring petrol prices
Article:
LOS ANGELES: At a gas station in Los Angeles, Ryder Thomas wore a grimace of barely suppressed anger as he filled his pickup truck, watching the cost tick up to US$130 for a full tank – US$30 more than he was paying before the US and Israel attacked Iran.
"I'm mad about the price, but I'm even madder about why it's so high," the 28-year-old told AFP.
This week, pump prices in the United States climbed to their highest level since early 2022, when Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine triggered a global surge in the cost of oil.
Hostilities in the Middle East, which began when Israel and the United States started bombing Iran on February 28, have crimped supply, sending the price of crude surging.
The war launched by President Donald Trump is unpopular all over the United States, with polls showing a clear majority disapprove of a conflict whose aims they think are unclear.
"There was absolutely no need for this war. It's just like when we invaded Iraq, there were no weapons of mass destruction," Thomas said.
"Trump is an idiot, that's all it is."
For weeks, the Republican billionaire has insisted that attacking Iran was necessary to prevent the country from acquiring nuclear weapons, and has repeatedly promised a short conflict.
But despite the overwhelming might of the US military, the Islamic republic has blocked the Strait of Hormuz – a global trade artery used to transport a fifth of the world's oil and gas.
DOMINO EFFECT
The result of this squeeze on supply is rising prices, sending gas in already-expensive California above US$6 a gallon (US$1.59 per liter), compared with around US$4.50 a gallon before the war.
Thomas worries about the domino effect this oil crunch will have on everything else he buys, as the rising cost of transport pushes up the prices of everyday goods like food and clothing.
"I hope the middle-of-the-road voters who got Trump elected start thinking about it and realize what he's done to them," he said of a president who campaigned on reducing costs for everyday Americans.
Trump voter David Chavez, who was waiting among dozens of cars lining up to refuel at Costco – a wholesale retailer whose prices are often a little lower than average – said it was not that simple.
"It is not nice; we don't want to pay more for gas," acknowledged the cameraman as he got his credit card ready to fill up his minivan.
However, "we don't know everything that happens behind closed doors. It's too easy to blame one person for all the problems."
FOOD BANK
Chavez said he voted for Democrats in the past, but switched to Trump because of former president Joe Biden's "poor handling of immigration and poor handling of the economy."
He accused oil giants of taking advantage of the current climate to artificially inflate prices, and pointed out that stricter environmental rules in California amp up the cost to motorists in the massive state.
Nonetheless, he admitted to being disappointed in Trump, but said no one is perfect.
"Trump still handles things a little bit better," he said.
In the same gas line, 73-year-old Flo, who did not want to give her full name, said she no longer knew what or who to believe on the reasons for rising gas prices.
What she knew for certain was that increased fuel costs were making her life much harder.
"With gas this high, I go to the food bank now, more so than ever," the retiree told AFP.
"I've had to cut back on driving too."
Between her pension and a part-time job she takes home around US$3,000 a month – almost half of which is swallowed by rent.
"Life was already hard," she sighed. "But now it's just harder." - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Poll: 61 pct of citizens say US conflict with Iran a mistake
Article:
WASHINGTON: A majority of United States (US) citizens believe their country's military use of force against Iran was a mistake, according to a new poll published on Friday, reported Xinhua.
Sixty-one per cent of US citizens say that the use of military force against Iran was a mistake, with fewer than two in ten believing that the US actions in Iran have been successful, according to the
Washington Post-ABC News-Ipsos
poll.
About four in ten say it has been unsuccessful, while another four in ten say it is "too soon to tell," according to the poll.
The conflict in Iran is "as unpopular among Americans as the Iraq War during the year of peak violence in 2006 and the Vietnam War in the early 1970s,"
The Washington Post
said.
The poll also noted that support for the conflict among Republicans remains high: 79 per cent say it was the right decision.
In addition, most US citizens believe that the military action against Iran carries potential risks.
Among them, 61 per cent think it would increase the risk of terrorism targeting US citizens, 60 per cent believe it would raise the likelihood of the US economy falling into recession, and 56 per cent say the actions increase the risk of weakening relationships with US allies. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Puteri Umno chief urges stronger support for M40 women, says group often overlooked
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Puteri Umno chief Datuk Nurul Amal Mohd Fauzi today urged the government to strengthen support for young women in the middle-income (M40) group, warning that many remain overlooked despite facing mounting economic and social pressures.
Speaking at the Umno 80th anniversary, she said the challenge facing Umno today is not to favour either older or younger generations, but to bridge the gap between them.
“This group is not merely carrying the burden of daily living, but is also balancing responsibilities and expectations,” she said.
“It is now time for the government not only to provide temporary relief, but to build an ecosystem that genuinely enables young women to grow and thrive without limitations.”
Nurul highlighted young M40 women as one such group, describing them as often perceived to be stable and resilient, yet quietly grappling with the strain of rising living costs and competing responsibilities.
She added that many in this group are not only managing their own livelihoods, but are also balancing career demands with family responsibilities and expectations.
“They may appear strong and composed, but beneath that, they are struggling to cope with increasing pressures,” she said.
She also called on the government to move beyond short-term assistance and instead develop a more comprehensive ecosystem that would enable young women to thrive.
Among the measures proposed were expanding access to income-generating opportunities to ensure more inclusive economic participation, as well as strengthening childcare support through affordable, safe and accessible nursery services, including at workplaces.
She also proposed increasing tax relief for each child from RM2,000 to RM3,000 to help ease financial burdens on families.
In addition, Nurul said the Budi Madani programme should be extended to private diesel vehicle owners who have also been affected by recent policy changes.
“These measures are important to ensure that young women are not caught between the demands of their careers and their responsibilities at home,” she said.

Title: Kota Bharu college offers support to family of teen student murdered in Ketereh, pledges full cooperation in police investigation
Article:
KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Kolej Poly-Tech Mara Sdn Bhd (KPTMSB) has expressed sorrow over the death of its 19-year-old student from its campus here who was found in Ketereh, Kelantan with multiple stab wounds yesterday.
The institution conveyed its condolences to the family of the victim, Nurfisya Zulkifly from Kuala Kangsar, Perak, who returned to campus a week ago for the new semester.
“KPTMSB is also providing assistance and full support to the victim’s family to help them through this difficult time,” it said in a statement on Facebook this afternoon.
The college said it is fully cooperating with the police amid the murder investigations.
Nurfisya Zulkifly was discovered dead by the roadside near a paddy field in Kampung Simah in Ketereh with 61 stab wounds on May 1, 2026, Kelantan police said.
She was buried in her hometown cemetery at 5.30am today.
The case is being investigated under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, as well as whipping upon conviction.
Four people are being questioned as suspects.
Two of them are teen boys, one whom police believe to be the victim’s boyfriend and the main perpetrator while the other is said to be the victim’s friend.
The other suspects are the alleged boyfriend’s parents.

Title: MetMalaysia warns of afternoon thunderstorms in all states, heavy rain and strong winds in Klang Valley
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — The Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning covering the Klang Valley and several other states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 6pm today.
Affected areas include Selangor, Kuala Lumpur, and Putrajaya, as well as multiple regions across Perlis, Kedah, Penang and Perak.
Other states under the alert include Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang, Negeri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor, covering several districts in each state.
In East Malaysia, the warning also extends to parts of Sarawak including Kuching, Samarahan, Sibu, Bintulu and Miri, as well as multiple divisions in Sabah including Kota Kinabalu, Penampang and Tuaran.
The alert also covers Labuan, with thunderstorms expected across all listed areas.
The MetMalaysia said thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected or occurring in areas where rainfall exceeds 20mm per hour and may last more than an hour.
It added that thunderstorm warnings are short-term advisories valid for up to six hours per issuance.

Title: Rain Rave festival debate should not be framed in extremes, says Johor DAP vice-chairman
Article:
KUCHING, May 2 — The debate over the Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 should not be reduced to extreme positions and must instead be approached rationally, balancing values, freedom and economic considerations, according to Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali.
In a statement posted on Facebook, Sheikh Umar said Malaysia’s position as a Muslim-majority country with a diverse society requires policy discussions to be handled in a measured manner rather than emotionally.
He said the issue should not be viewed through a “black-and-white” lens of either total prohibition or complete freedom.
“Instead, we need to look at how any policy or activity impacts the overall well-being of society,” he said.
Sheikh Umar stressed that safeguarding religious values remains important, adding that concerns raised by the Muslim community over public morality and conduct in public spaces should be respected.
However, he said Malaysia is also part of a global ecosystem in tourism, economy and culture, and events such as the Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 should be considered within the context of a competitive tourism economy.
He said such events have the potential to attract international tourists, boost the hospitality and services sectors, and create economic opportunities for locals.
“Denying all of this entirely is also not a realistic approach in today’s world,” he said.
At the same time, he said moral and dignified living is a universal value shared across faiths, and that freedom must come with responsibility.
He added that the way forward is not outright bans or unrestricted freedom, but managed freedom guided by clear values.
Among the principles he outlined were freedom with responsibility, clear and structured regulatory space, consistent enforcement of rules, and education that helps society make informed choices rather than relying solely on enforcement.
Sheikh Umar also said Malaysia must mature in its understanding of majority rule and minority rights, stressing that while the majority shapes national direction, minority rights must still be protected.
He said Malaysia should not be forced to choose between being a principled nation and an economically competitive one, but instead must strive to be both — stable, moderate and globally trusted.
“Malaysia must continue moving forward as a nation grounded in values, but not afraid of progress,” he said.

Title: Police chase on NKVE ends with Proton Preve ramming BMW in Subang, fleeing driver has criminal and drug records
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — A local man and a foreign woman were arrested last night in Petaling Jaya after the car they were travelling in rammed into another vehicle while trying to flee a police checkpoint.
Petaling Jaya police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said a search of the suspects’ car also revealed 17 transparent packets containing suspected drugs, weighing around 16 grammes.
He said a report was received at 9.08 pm about a patrol car chasing a Proton Preve along the New Klang Valley Expressway, near the Subang Toll Plaza exit heading towards Shah Alam, after the vehicle had tried to evade inspection.
“The 45-year-old male suspect drove dangerously, entered the Subang Jaya intersection, and crashed into a black BMW at the traffic lights on Persiaran Tujuan in Subang Jaya, before police finally managed to apprehend him at the scene,” Shamsudin said in a statement today.
He added that the male suspect has 21 past criminal and drug-related records, while the female suspect has no prior record.
“The male suspect tested positive for methamphetamine. Both suspects have been remanded for three days, until May 4,” he said.
Shamsudin added that the investigation is being carried out under Section 186 of the Penal Code for obstructing a civil servant in the discharge of their duties, Sections 12(2) and Section 39A(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 for drug possession, and Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
He urged anyone with information regarding the incident to come forward to the nearest police station or to contact the Petaling Jaya District Police Headquarters’ operations room at 03-79662222. — Bernama

Title: Kicked, dragged, dunked in water: Four teen boys under police questioning after report of bullying at Tanjung Kling orphanage
Article:
MELAKA, May 2 — Police detained four male students to assist in the investigation into a bullying case involving a resident at an orphanage in Tanjung Kling, here.
Melaka Police Chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said police received a report on April 29 at about 7.11 pm from the victim’s mother, who alleged that her son, a student residing at Rumah Anak Yatim Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Al Haj Pantai Puteri, had been bullied by senior students known to him.
He said the victim was believed to have been assaulted, kicked, had his head submerged into a bucket filled with water, and dragged across the toilet floor by the suspects.
“Following the report, a team of officers and personnel from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Melaka Tengah District Police Headquarters detained four male students aged between 15 and 16 on April 30 at about 3.45 pm,” he said in a statement today.
According to him, all the suspects are secondary school students with no prior records, and urine screening tests returned negative results.
Dzulkhairi said all four suspects were released on police bail after their statements were recorded to assist further investigation.
The case is being investigated under Section 507B of the Penal Code, which provides for imprisonment of up to three years or a fine or both, upon conviction.
Meanwhile, he advised the public not to speculate or spread unverified information regarding the case.
Police also urged the public to seek verification from authorities before sharing any information on social media and assured that they remain committed to ensuring public safety and order at all times. — Bernama

Title: Sabah electricity bills rising? Energy company says aircon use likeliest reason as tariff unchanged
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 2 — Sabah Electricity has acknowledged growing public concern over higher electricity bills in recent weeks, following claims on social media questioning whether the increase was due to higher tariff rates.
Its chief executive officer, Datuk Mohd Yaakob Jaafar, said the utility company understands the financial strain faced by consumers amid rising living costs and takes seriously all feedback received.
He stressed that the increase in electricity bills is largely driven by higher energy consumption rather than any changes in tariff rates.
“The rise in electricity bills is influenced by several key factors, including increased usage due to prolonged hot weather, which has led to more frequent use of cooling appliances such as air conditioners,” he said in a statement issued on Saturday after attending the Sabah Electricity Kaamatan Chess Festival 2026 on Friday.
He added that modern lifestyles, which rely heavily on electrical appliances and digital devices, have also contributed to higher energy consumption in households and business premises.

Title: Immigration swoops on Kuching premises, detains 17 foreigners in crackdown on undocumented workers
Article:
KUCHING, May 2 — The Immigration Department (JIM) in Sarawak arrested 17 foreigners in an operation conducted around the city yesterday.
JIM Sarawak, in a statement, announced that of the total, 16 individuals were Indonesians, while the others were Pakistani nationals.
“All those detained were found to have failed to produce valid passports or travel documents during inspections carried out at several premises, including shared houses and rented rooms,” said JIM Sarawak.
According to the statement, the operation involving 28 enforcement officers also included a comprehensive inspection of locations identified as foreign residences.
“This operation aims to detect and take action against foreigners who violate the provisions of the immigration law,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Abang Johari leads Sarawak delegation to UK, Ireland in push for AI, data centres and semiconductor growth
Article:
KUCHING, May 2 — Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg is leading an official working visit to Manchester and Bristol, United Kingdom (UK), and Dublin, Ireland, starting today until May 5.
The Sarawak Public Communications Unit (UKAS) in a statement announced that the visit is a strategic mission in an effort to strengthen the state’s data centre infrastructure, artificial intelligence (AI) technology, semiconductors, and talent development.
“In Dublin, Abang Johari is scheduled to visit the K2 Data Centre, while in Manchester, he will participate in the Sarawak Agenda-Townhall programme in addition to attending a dinner and gathering with Sarawakians.
“In addition, Abang Johari will also hold an engagement session with representatives of the Manchester Regional Government at the British Muslim Heritage Centre,” said UKAS.
According to the statement, in Bristol, Abang Johari is scheduled to attend an engagement session between SMD Semiconductor and the University of Bristol and deliver a speech at the event.
“Abang Johari will also witness the exchange of a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) involving SMD Semiconductor in addition to visiting the National Composites Centre (NCC),” it said. — Bernama

Title: After withdrawing support, Negeri Sembilan Umno says MB’s fate now with BN, PH leadership
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Negeri Sembilan Umno will defer to party leadership on its next course of action following its withdrawal of support for the menteri besar, including any decision on a potential replacement, its chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said today.
Jalaluddin said the state chapter would abide by decisions made by Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) leadership, including whether the next menteri besar should come from outside Umno.
“We will wait and see what the leadership decides, be it BN or PH. It is not for Negeri Sembilan Umno to determine,” he told reporters when met outside Merdeka Hall at the World Trade Centre.
He stressed that the current political crisis was “extraordinary”, following the decision by 14 Umno assemblymen on April 27 to withdraw support for the menteri besar.
Jalaluddin said the move came after issues the group could no longer accept, adding that the crisis was triggered by what he described as mismanagement by the menteri besar that disrupted the state’s political harmony.
“This is not about politics, power or ‘tebuk atap’ (back door). This concerns governance and the institutions of adat and the Negeri Sembilan monarchy,” he said.
He added that the loss of confidence by the 14 assemblymen was final and not a shifting political stance.
“If we have pulled support and the menteri besar continues to govern, it means we have lost trust,” he said.
Jalaluddin reiterated that Umno Negeri Sembilan is prepared to take on the role of opposition if directed by party leadership, potentially until the next general election.
“What we will do next depends on Umno’s decision. We don’t mind being in the opposition and if we are to be in the opposition, we will remain so until GE16,” he said.
On speculation over a replacement menteri besar, including the possibility of a PKR candidate, he said the matter was premature.
He warned that public debate surrounding the issue risked creating confusion, particularly among younger Malaysians, and could harm the reputation of Negeri Sembilan’s adat institutions.
“The question now is not about individuals or positions, but about the responsibility of a menteri besar. We cannot allow a crisis like this to happen, especially one involving adat,” he said.
“When such matters are debated openly, many will not fully understand adat. This can lead to negative perceptions and confusion among youths and students,” he added.
Jalaluddin said Umno was compelled to act to prevent further damage.
Earlier in his speech, Jalaluddin said many had accused Umno of betraying the government, but they forget that in 2023, Umno Negeri Sembilan won 14 seats — the most among all parties.
In the 2023 Negeri Sembilan state polls, Umno secured 14 seats, followed by PKR (five), DAP (11), Perikatan Nasional (five), and Amanah (one).
Jalaluddin pointed out that with the strongest representation at the time, he could have moved to destabilise the government earlier if he had intended to do so.
“If I wanted to stir something, I could have done it then but I respected the federal leadership’s call to maintain peace and stability so that we could work together,” he said.
He said Umno had continued to play its role within the state government over the past two-and-a-half years, including holding four executive council (exco) positions.
Rejecting accusations of a “tebuk atap” (backdoor takeover), Jalaluddin said such claims were misplaced.
“Tebuk atap is when you try to take over the government from outside. We are already part of the government — we have exco positions and allocations. What ‘tebuk atap’ is this?” he asked.
He stressed that the withdrawal of support was prompted by dissatisfaction with the state government’s handling of a recent crisis, which he said could not be compromised.
“We are also close to the general election, but we cannot compromise when there is failure in governance,” he said.
Jalaluddin said Umno Negeri Sembilan would now sit in the opposition for as long as it believes the state government is not performing effectively.
He added that the party would focus on strengthening its position ahead of the next general election.
“We will do our best to win all seats. We do not need to cater to others’ feelings. As chairman of the state Umno machinery, it is my responsibility to ensure we are in a dominant position,” he said.
He also rejected claims that Umno’s actions were motivated by a desire for power or positions.
“Umno Negeri Sembilan is not greedy or hungry for posts. We did not want to disrupt the momentum, but we acted because we have principles we cannot abandon — integrity, and our promises to religion and nation,” he said.
Reaffirming the party’s stance, Jalaluddin ended with a firm message: “No retreat, no surrender.”

Title: Jealous rage likely motive behind Ketereh college girl’s killing, Kelantan police say amid week-long remand for boyfriend and buddy
Article:
KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Jealousy is believed to be the motive behind the murder of a 19-year-old college student whose body was found with 61 stab wounds in Keterah yesterday.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investigators suspect the victim was stabbed to death by her 19-year-old boyfriend, who allegedly believed she was having an affair, the
New Straits Times
reported today.
“Initial investigations showed that an argument broke out between the victim and the suspect, which led to her death.
“She is believed to have been stabbed while inside the suspect’s car,” the senior policeman was quoted as saying.
He said the boyfriend, identified as the main suspect, was arrested yesterday alongside another youth believed to be a friend of the victim.
Both are now under a seven-day remand order.
The policeman added that the boyfriend’s parents — a 66-year-old man and a 60-year-old woman – have also been detained for the next three days to assist in investigations.
The remand orders were issued by Magistrate Amirul Asyraf Abdul Rasid today, the
New Straits Times
reported.
Mohd Yusoff confirmed all suspects have no prior criminal records and tested negative for drugs.
He said investigations into the case are ongoing under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder, which carry the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, as well as whipping upon conviction.
Local media had earlier identified the victim as 19-year-old Nurfisya Zulkifly from Kuala Kangsar, Perak who had just returned to her Ketereh college campus to start a new semester a week ago.
Her body with 61 stab wounds was found by the roadside near a paddy field in Kampung Simah yesterday and was buried early this morning in her hometown.

Title: One day, two fatal falls at KLIA2: Police identify second foreigner as 30-year-old Algerian man
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 2 — Police have confirmed another fatal incident of a foreigner after falling at Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2 (KLIA T2), last night.
KLIA district police chief ACP M. Ravi said in the incident at about 9.10 pm, a 30-year-old Algerian man is believed to have fallen from the outside of the balcony at the terminal.
He said the medical team provided emergency treatment at the scene before the victim was taken to the Cyberjaya Hospital for further treatment.
“However, at about 2.55 am this morning, the hospital confirmed that the man had died,” he said in a statement today.
The case is classified as a Sudden Death Report (SDR) and the cause of the incident is still being investigated
Anyone with information is asked to contact the KLIA district police headquarters at 03-8776 8222 to assist in the investigation.
Yesterday afternoon, police reported that a woman from China fell to her death from Level 3 of Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 2. — Bernama

Title: Regular play spot but not her slippers, says mum of missing nine-year-old girl swept into Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
Article:
KAJANG, May 2 — A nine-year-old girl, feared drowned after falling into a drain and swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras yesterday, often played in the area where the incident occurred.
Her mother, Nordina Zulkarnain, 34, said the open area next to the apartment was a popular spot for local children to play in, including her daughter Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum.
“She was playing there yesterday because it was a public holiday, hanging out with her friends from the apartment.
“After it happened, my second son, her brother, rushed home and told my husband and me,” she told reporters at the scene here today.
Nordina said her husband then immediately called emergency services for fire department assistance.
On the slippers and other items found in today’s search area, she confirmed they were not her daughter’s.
Day Two of a search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026. — Bernama pic
Meanwhile, the victim’s grandfather, Zulkarnain Ahmad, 57, who rushed from Seremban to the scene yesterday, said he could not sleep at all since yesterday.
“Today I’m joining the rescue teams searching the area,” he said.
A search and rescue for a nine-year-old girl who was swept away by strong currents in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, Selangor on May 1, 2026, expands into the underground drainage system on Day Two.— Bernama pic
Media reports yesterday cited that a nine-year-old girl fell into a drain while chasing a ball, before being swept away by strong currents.
Firefighters received the emergency call about the incident at Sri Hijau Condominium at 7.08 pm. — Bernama

Title: DBKL imposes 300m buffer zone on Bukit Kiara project after TTDI residents’ concerns, says Hannah Yeoh
Article:
